item.title: #DevDiscuss：WebAssembly 的使用 | #DevDiscuss: Uses of WebAssembly
item.description:
开发团队的Erin Bensinger

发表于 1 月 10 日

受@cocoandrew的 Top 7 帖子启发，今晚的主题是……WebAssembly 的使用！

问题：

  • 根据您的经验，WebAssembly 的最佳用途是什么？
  • 什么时候应该避免使用 WebAssembly？
  • 您有任何关于 WebAssembly 成功和/或失败的故事吗？

item.title: 过去一周的 7 大精选 DEV 帖子 | Top 7 Featured DEV Posts from the Past Week
item.description:
开发团队的Michael Tharrington（他/他）

发表于 1 月 10 日

每个星期二，我们都会根据流量、参与度和一些编辑策划来汇总前一周的热门帖子。 典型的一周从星期一开始到星期日结束，但不用担心，我们会考虑本周晚些时候发布的帖子。

用图像理解 Git

受 Nico Riedmann 的“学习 git 概念，而不是命令”的启发， @nopenoshishi整理了一份很棒的 Git 系统结构指南，其中包含手绘图像！

提高面试机会

找工作总是一个艰难的经历。 在这里， @andrewbaisden描述了您可能采取的许多行动，以确保您踏上 IT 业务的大门。

数据库 101

对于那些没有探索过数据库的人来说，数据库可能是一个令人困惑和恐惧的话题。 减少 Docker 图像大小

在他们的宠物项目中将 Docker 镜像的大小减少了 97%， @kitarp29发现了一些真正有效的方法来降低 Docker 镜像的大小。 继续阅读，看看他们是如何做到的！

在 CSS 中绘制徽标插图是学习语言的来龙去脉的好方法。 在这篇文章中， @ratracegrad 将带领我们创建 Google 徽标。

在本文中， @cocoandrew探讨了如何在 Web 应用程序中使用 WebAssembly。

开发者博客

虽然开始起来可能很困难，但@nasirovelchin提出了博客对软件开发人员特别有益的几个原因。

这就是我们本周二的每周前 7 名！ 请关注本周的 dev.to 以获取每日内容和讨论……并确保在未来关注本系列。 你可能只是在其中！

安东尼奥迪莫塔 - 1月10日

原文:

Michael Tharrington (he/him) for The DEV Team

Posted on Jan 10

Every Tuesday we round up the previous week's top posts based on traffic, engagement, and a hint of editorial curation. The typical week starts on Monday and ends on Sunday, but don't worry, we take into account posts that are published later in the week.

Understanding Git with Images

Inspired by Nico Riedmann's "Learn git concepts, not commands", @nopenoshishi put together an awesome guide on Git’s system structure featuring hand drawn-images!

Improve Your Chances at Interviews

Looking for work is always a tough experience. Here, @andrewbaisden describes many actions you might take to make sure you get your foot in the door of the IT business.

Databases 101

Databases can be a confusing and scary topic for those who haven’t explored them. @danielhe4rt shares their learnings, letting us know what they find particularly fascinating about databases.

Reduce Docker Image Sizes

Reducing the size of the Docker image in their pet project by 97%, @kitarp29 uncovered some really effective methods for bringing down the size of Docker images. Read on to see how they made it happen!

Google Logo in CSS

Illustrating logos in CSS is a great way to learn the ins and outs of the language. In this post, @ratracegrad walks us through creating the Google logo.

A Guide to WebAssembly

WebAssembly (Wasm) is a low-level binary format that is designed to be faster and more efficient than traditional JavaScript. In this article, @cocoandrew explores how to use WebAssembly in a web application.

Blogging for Developers

Though it can be difficult to get going, @nasirovelchin puts forth several reasons why blogging is particularly beneficial to software developers.

That's it for our weekly Top 7 for this Tuesday! Keep an eye on dev.to this week for daily content and discussions...and be sure to keep an eye on this series in the future. You might just be in it!

Antonio Di Motta - Jan 10

item.title: 创建令人惊叹的 GitHub 配置文件自述文件：提示和技巧 | Creating a Stunning GitHub Profile README: Tips and Tricks
item.description:
您可以通过创建出色的 GitHub 配置文件 README 向潜在雇主、合作者和其他开源社区成员展示您的工作和能力。 下面列出了一些帮助您使 README 独一无二的想法：

使用独特且引人注目的标题。 你的 GitHub 配置文件的主要目标应该在你的标题中用一个简洁的句子来概括。

添加一个简洁的简历。 用几句话告诉人们你是谁，你在做什么。 包括所有相关的详细信息，例如您目前的职位或专业领域。
你可以看到我的 - github.com/Gauraw007

创建一个整洁且结构合理的布局。 通过使用标题、要点和图形来分解文本，使您的 README 易于阅读。

展示你最好的作品。 利用图片、GIF 和视频来突出您最迷人的项目和创意作品，人们将能够看到您的能力以及您对什么充满热情。

包括号召性用语 为了鼓励人们更多地了解您和您的工作，请包括指向您的网站、博客或其他在线资料的链接。

利用小部件和徽章。 例如，要显示贡献者数量或存储库数量等信息，您可以使用 GitHub 统计信息等 GitHub 小部件。

展示你的能力。 在您的 README 中使用标签或关键字来表达您的专业领域。

在您的 README 中添加一些 markdown 和 HTML，使其成为您自己的。 小心为您的个人资料选择合适的字体和配色方案，使其看起来很有吸引力。 通过使用本文中的建议，您可以创建一个脱颖而出的 GitHub 个人资料 README，并有效地向潜在的雇主和合作者宣传您的工作和能力。 这里有一些 Github Repos，用于添加额外的功能

盾牌（有小徽章）- github.com/badges/shields


添加 gihubstack - github.com/anuraghazra/github-readme-st ...
添加您的文章 - github.com/bxcodec/github-readme-medium ...

如果 gaurav_meherkhamb 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区 👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 gaurav_meherkhamb：

原文:

You may show off your work and abilities to potential employers, collaborators, and other open-source community members by creating a stellar GitHub profile README. Listed below are some ideas to help you make your README unique:

Use a title that is distinct and compelling. Your GitHub profile's main objective should be summed up in a single, succinct sentence in your title.

Add a succinct bio. Tell folks who you are and what you do in a few sentences. Include all pertinent details, such as your present position or area of expertise.
you can see mine - github.com/Gauraw007

Create a neat and well-structured layout. Make your README easy to read by using headings, bullet points, and graphics to break up the text.

Feature your best work. Utilize images, GIFs, and videos to highlight your most fascinating projects and creative work People will be able to see what you are capable of and what you are enthusiastic about as a result.

Including a call to action To encourage people to discover more about you and your work, include links to your website, blog, or other online profiles.

Utilize widgets and badges. To display information like the number of contributors or the number of repositories, for example, you can use GitHub widgets like GitHub stats.

Showcase your abilities. Use tags or keywords in your README that express your areas of expertise.

Add some markdown and HTML to your README to make it your own. Make careful to pick the appropriate font and color scheme for your profile so that it looks appealing. You can make a GitHub profile README that stands out and effectively advertises your work and abilities to potential employers and collaborators by using the advice in this article. Here are Some Github Repos, for adding extra features

shield(have small badges) - github.com/badges/shields


Adding gihubstack - github.com/anuraghazra/github-readme-st…
Add your articles - github.com/bxcodec/github-readme-medium…

Harinder Seera 🇭🇲 - Jan 4

NaveenKumar Namachivayam ⚡ - Jan 3

Dany Paredes - Dec 30 '22

item.title: 如何定位您的团队以在估算中取得成功 | How to Position Your Team for Success in Estimation
item.description:
如果您想成功完成项目，估算是至关重要的。 如果您从错误的估算方法开始，该项目几乎肯定会失败。 为了产生更有希望的估计，将每个估计过程问题导向可重复的标准过程。 聪明的方法可以降低不确定性。 在处理售前阶段时，拥有最精确的估算结果可以帮助您处理项目计划。 这也有助于流程更成功地运行，尤其是在面临紧迫的时间表和偏差的危险时。

专家团队必须参与估算过程和项目的早期阶段。 通过拥有可靠的工作人员，团队可以做出更准确的估计。 无论使用何种方法或开发生命周期，估算方法必须是团队在每个项目开始时进行的可重复过程。 还强烈建议团队在每个阶段后参加“经验教训”会议，利用本次会议期间产生的想法和选择为下一步做准备。 必须安排这些会议来审查团队之前创建的估算，并纳入在先前阶段或具有类似结构的过去项目中未考虑的新因素。 这种做法将帮助团队增强他们对未来的预测。

停止、开始和继续方法是提供或征求反馈的宝贵框架，通常用于生成这些“经验教训”。 它包括个人应该坚持做的三件事，应该停止做的三件事，以及应该开始做的三件事。 反馈对于评估当前的绩效水平以及提出改进建议至关重要。 不幸的是，大多数人都不擅长提供或接收反馈。 停止、开始和继续技术是一种简单而有效的范例，用于克服与提供和接收反馈相关的一些困难。

这个坚实的基础提醒您，在开始每个项目阶段之前，您必须评估值得付出的努力。 例如，某些测试活动被忽略，并假设不会有任何难以解决的问题。 但是当测试团队开始工作时，它了解到某些测试用例需要比其他测试用例多一点的工作。 技术问题、初始策略中未考虑的新替代流程以及其他因素可能是造成这种复杂性的原因。

团队通常会放弃对导致极低或极小开发工作的修订的测试工作。 然而，任何调整，无论多么小，都可能对产品质量产生负面影响。 在每个测试过程之后，开发团队必须解决发现的任何问题。 这项工作经常被低估，导致项目预算超支。 运行多个循环后，您的努力可能会成功衡量和改进。

从团队的角度来看，在估算过程中很容易忽略组织关注点。 这些担忧可能与共享资源、知识管理和外部危险等更密切相关。 尽管如此，尽管这些困难可能会影响估计的结果，但团队必须通过仅关注任务努力来进行估计。 因此，组织困难必须作为风险管理过程的一部分加以解决，并且必须与估计结果一起反映在项目计划中。

这是免费的在线随机颜色生成器，可从调色板中随机选取一种颜色。

缓冲区拆解

大多数时候，团队成员会在初始估算的基础上增加一些额外的工作或缓冲，以使其更加灵活。 这是一个可怕的习惯。 评估必须是诚实的，每个特定的挑战都公开展示给所有团队成员进行评估。 每个小组成员必须控制相同的概念和假设。 任何差异都会产生不可信的结果。 在任何估算会议开始之前，必须有明确的指导方针和规范，以防止使用不准确的数字，例如：

  • 没有停顿、周末或节假日。 我们将在不对时间表或截止日期做出任何假设的情况下估算工作量。”

  • 估算结果必须简单地根据工作量或复杂性提供衡量标准，而不考虑喝咖啡的时间、潜在风险或缓冲。 项目计划和进度表必须包括风险、中断和缓冲。 要根据共享的概念和假设取得成果，每个团队成员都必须了解这些准则。

    未计算所有因素的估计

    当团队计算估计工作量时，他们需要检查冲刺承诺中涉及的所有因素。 这些因素应该明确定义为团队在估算过程中要遵循的清单。 这是一个示例清单：

    • 对其他团队有依赖性吗？

    • 谁可以完成任务？

    • 这些人的经验和知识是什么？

    • 是否存在任何可能影响时间表的已知风险？

    • 什么是开发和测试策略？

    顺应团队中的“专家”

    估算过程涉及所有团队成员。 他们是平等的，可以在估算过程中分享他们的反馈。 当团队成员缺乏信心提供他们的估计并因此服从一个强大的团队成员的意见时，问题就开始了。 如果我们想要建立一个跨职能团队，我们必须确保每个团队成员都能为整体工作做出贡献并分享他们的反馈。

    解决这个问题的一个好方法是让团队中的专家不进行估算，而让团队中的其他人进行估算。 全部完成后，我们可以请专家与团队其他成员分享他们的估计。

    另外，请查看此免费的在线随机日期生成器工具，该工具可让您生成随机日历日期。

    在不沟通的情况下调整估计

    团队进行了他们的计划会议，给出了估计，然后团队开始了冲刺。 他们开始编写一个故事，然后突然意识到他们最初的估计是错误的（即设想的解决方案是不正确的）。 团队根据他们现在掌握的新信息在冲刺期间调整最初的估计，但没有告诉任何人。 这表示团队中的主要问题，在它们对团队的执行能力产生更大影响之前，您必须了解这些问题（例如，团队内部害怕出错、估计不够有效或使用不当）。

    分享不同的观点

    允许个人表达自己的想法是带领团队达成一致的绝佳策略。 当两项或多项措施之间存在冲突或存在显着差异时，必须让团队成员表达自己的观点并解释他们是如何得出具体结论的。 这个概念用于多种技术，包括 Scrum 计划扑克和极限编程。 这是确保团队对最终结果感到满意并且没有未解决问题的最有效方法。 在讨论了差异并给出了成员的观点之后，必须再次估计有问题的点，直到团队达成一致。

    从现在开始，区分领导者和推动者是至关重要的。 前者会迫使团队接受他的估计，将其他团队成员排除在决策过程之外。 这种微观管理风格很少成功。 另一方面，促进者将让团队参与决策，并督促成员进行评估，使评估更加诚实和成功。

    完成的定义 (DoD)

    进行有效估算时的另一个考虑因素是就“完成”的含义达成一致。 为准确估计工作量，团队必须了解某事何时完成以及必须完成哪些项目才能到达那里。 完成的定义 (DoD) 应该是任何涉及不同利益相关者的敏捷项目的主要起点之一。 产品负责人和团队应始终共同努力，就明确的完成定义达成一致，该定义将定义故事或功能是否已准备好作为增量交付。

    对于如何确定 DoD 有不同的解释。 一种常见的做法是声明一旦进行了软件测试，开发就完成了。 在这种情况下，团队只是说，“只有当团队中的测试人员说完成时，故事才算完成”。 如果那是
    你决定如何在你的团队中做到这一点，你必须确保测试人员是产品负责人的焦点，这样团队才能理解 PO 的意图。

    对我来说，由于以下几个原因，这种测试人员批准的方法不太有效：

    • 单点故障。

    • 开发人员可能会忽略他们的责任。 它创建了测试人员和开发人员的分离。

    实施 DoD 的更常见（也更有效）的方法是使用清单，这些清单指定完成功能、冲刺或故事所需的标准和要求。 让我们看几个例子：

    功能的完成定义可由以下标准确定：

    • 与此功能相关的所有基本故事均已获得客户批准。

    • 有一个完整、稳定的工作版本可供发布。

    • 所有的bug都在技术上得到解决，或者在PO的认可下处于可接受的状态。

    • 功能文档完整，包括用户手册、发行说明和已知问题。

    开发冲刺的完成定义可由以下标准确定：

    • 冲刺目标已完成。

    • 所有用户故事都已完成并获得批准。

    • 发布说明已编写并记录在案。

    • 编写、执行并通过了自动化测试。

    用户故事的完成定义可由以下标准确定：

    • 团队已经确定、估计和批准了实施和测试所选故事所需的所有任务。

    • 与故事相关的所有错误都已报告并验证。

    • 所有编码和测试活动都已完成。

    • 添加到 sprint backlog 中的每个故事都得到了团队的充分理解和批准，并且具有用户故事的所有元素，包括验收标准、验收测试等。

    • 代码已集成到主分支中。

    这是另一个可以帮助您生成随机 guid 生成器的免费在线收费。您可以使用 GUID 代码序列化 Windows 和 Mac 软件或为您的在线商店创建优惠券代码。

    问题管理

    最后，在估算项目时，要记住的最重要的事情之一是尽可能多地划分范围内的活动。 如果您必须估算建筑物的高度，您可能不知道从哪里开始。 但是，如果您弄清楚需要砌多少堵墙以及每平方米需要多少块砖，您就能在谈判中处于更有利的地位，从而准确地起草项目计划。

    当工作组件较小时，您可以更好地控制这个现在可以衡量的困难。 您可能不知道如何为摩天大楼报价，但您必须知道一块砖需要多少钱。



原文:

Estimates are critical if you want to be successful with projects. If you begin with a bad estimating approach, the project will almost certainly fail. To produce a much more promising estimate, direct each estimation-process issue toward a repeatable standard process. A smart approach reduces the degree of uncertainty. When dealing with presales phases, having the most precise estimation findings can assist you to deal with the project plan. This also helps the process to function more successfully, especially when faced with tight schedules and the danger of deviation.

A team of specialists must be included in both the estimating process and the early stages of the project. By having a reliable crew, the teams can make more accurate estimates. The estimating approach must be a repeatable process that the team undertakes at the start of each project, regardless of the methodology or development lifecycle used. It is also strongly advised that the team attend a “lessons learned” meeting after each stage, with the ideas and choices generated during this session utilized to prepare for the following step. These meetings must be scheduled to go over the estimate that the team created earlier, incorporating new factors that were not considered in prior phases or past projects with a similar structure. This practice will help the team enhance their future forecasts.

The Stop, Start, and continue the approach, which is a valuable framework for offering or asking for feedback, is typically used to generate these “lessons learnt.” It includes three things the individual should maintain doing, three things they should cease doing, and three new things they should start doing. Feedback is critical for assessing current levels of performance as well as suggesting areas for improvement. Unfortunately, most individuals are not great at either providing or receiving feedback. The Stop, Start and continue technique is a simple and effective paradigm for overcoming some of the difficulties associated with both delivering and receiving feedback.

This strong foundation serves as a reminder that before beginning each project stage, you must assess the worthwhile efforts. For instance, certain testing activities are ignored with the presumption that there won’t be any difficult problems to resolve. But when the test team gets down to business, it learns that certain test cases call for a little more work than others. Technology problems, fresh alternate flows that weren’t taken into account in the initial strategy, and other factors may be to blame for this complexity.

It is also typical for teams to dismiss testing efforts for revisions that result in extremely low or minor development efforts. However, any adjustment, no matter how little, might have a negative influence on product quality. After each testing process, the development team must address any problems that were discovered. This work is frequently underestimated, resulting in over-budget projects. After running numerous cycles, your efforts may be successfully measured and improved.

It is easy to overlook organizational concerns in the estimating process from the perspective of the team. These concerns may be more closely tied to shared resources, knowledge management, and external dangers, among other things. Nonetheless, although these difficulties may have an impact on the estimation’s outcome, the team must conduct the estimation by focusing solely on task efforts. Thus, organizational difficulties must be addressed as part of the risk-management process, and they must be reflected in the project plan alongside the estimated results.

Here’s Free online random color generator which randomly picks a color from a color palette.

Buffer Teardown

The majority of the time, team members will add a little amount of additional work, or a buffer, to the initial estimation to make it more flexible. This is a terrible habit. The estimation must be honest, with every specific challenge openly shown for all team members to evaluate. Each group member must control the same concepts and assumptions. Any disparity will yield untrustworthy outcomes. There must be clear guidelines and norms in place before any estimating session begins to prevent the use of inaccurate numbers, for example:

  • “We’re going to estimate efforts assuming one person is working on each activity constantly.” There are no pauses, weekends, or holidays. We will estimate efforts without making any assumptions about a schedule or deadline.”

  • “We will not employ buffers to store deviations.”

  • “We’re going to estimate efforts rather than risks.”

  • “We’ll estimate in terms of man-hours,” or “we’ll estimate based on complexities.”

Estimation results must simply offer measures in terms of effort or complexities, without accounting for coffee breaks, potential risks, or buffers. The project plan and schedules must include risks, interruptions, and buffers. To achieve results based on shared concepts and assumptions, each team member must understand these guidelines.

Estimation Made Without Calculating All Factors

When the team calculates the estimated work, they need to examine all factors involved in their sprint commitments. These factors should be well defined as a checklist for the team to follow as part of their estimation process. Here is a sample checklist:

  • Are there any dependencies on other teams?

  • Who are the people available to accomplish the task?

  • What is the experience and knowledge of those people?

  • Are there any known risks that can affect the timelines?

  • What is the development and testing strategy?

Conforming to the “Expert” on the Team

The process of estimations involves all team members. They are equal and can share their feedback as part of the estimation process. The problem begins when team members lack the confidence to provide their estimation and therefore conform to the opinion of a strong team member. If we want to build a cross-functional team, we must ensure each team member can contribute to the overall effort and share their feedback.

A good way to resolve this is for the expert in the team not to estimate and have the rest of the team estimate. Once they are all done, we can ask the expert to share their estimate with the rest of the team.

Also, check out this Free online random date generator tool that allows you to generate random calendar dates.

Adjusting Estimates Without Communicating

The team had their planning session, estimates were given, and the team starts the sprint. They start work on a story and suddenly realize that their original estimates were wrong (i.e., the solution envisioned is incorrect). The team adjusts the original estimates during the sprint based on the new information they now have, without telling anyone. This indicates major problems within the team that you must understand before they have a bigger impact on the team’s ability to perform (e.g., fear within the team of being wrong, the estimation is not effective enough, or being used incorrectly).

Sharing Different Points of View

Allowing individuals to express their thoughts is an excellent strategy to lead the team to an agreement. When there are conflicts or a significant difference between two or more measures, it is essential to allow team members to express their points of view and explain how they arrived at specific conclusions. This concept is used in several techniques, including Scrum planning poker, and Extreme Programming. It is the most effective method for ensuring that the team is pleased with the final results and that there are no unresolved issues. After the discrepancies have been discussed and members’ viewpoints have been given, the point in question must be estimated again until the team reaches an agreement.

It’s critical to distinguish between a leader and an enabler from now on. The former will force the team to accept his estimate, excluding other team members from the decision-making process. This style of micromanagement is rarely successful. The facilitator, on the other hand, will include the team in decision-making and will urge members to do the evaluation, making the estimation more honest and successful.

Definition of Done (DoD)

Another consideration when doing effective estimations is agreeing on what “done” implies. To accurately estimate efforts, the team must understand when something is complete and what items must be accomplished to get there. The Definition of Done (DoD) should be one of the main starting points for any Agile projects that involve different stakeholders. The Product Owner and the team should always work together to agree on a clear Definition of Done which will define whether a story or feature is ready as an increment delivery.

There are different interpretations of how to determine the DoD. One common way to do it is to state that once the software testing is conducted, the development is finished. In this case, the team just says, “A story is completed only when the tester in the team says it’s done”. If that is
how you decide to do it in your team, you must ensure the tester is the focal point for the Product Owner, so the team understands the PO’s intentions.

For me, this method of tester approval is less effective for several reasons:

  • Single point of failure.

  • Developers may ignore their responsibilities. (Why shouldn’t they if they can always blame the tester?)

  • It creates a separation of testers and developers.

The more common (and far more effective) way to implement DoD is to use checklists that specify the criteria and requirements needed for completing a feature, sprint, or story. Let us look at a few examples:

The Definition of Done for a feature may be determined by the following criteria:

  • All essential stories relevant to this feature are approved by the customer.

  • There is a full, stable working version ready for release.

  • All bugs are resolved technically, or in an acceptable state with the approval of the PO.

  • Feature documentation is complete including user manuals, release notes, and known issues.

The Definition of Done for a development sprint may be determined by the following criteria:

  • The sprint goal is accomplished.

  • All user stories are completed and approved.

  • Release notes are written and documented.

  • Automated tests were written, executed, and passed.

The Definition of Done for a user story may be determined by the following criteria:

  • All tasks necessary to implement and test the selected story have been identified, estimated, and approved by the team.

  • All bugs associated with the story have been reported and verified.

  • All coding and testing activities are complete.

  • Every story added to the sprint backlog is fully understood, approved by the team, and has all the elements of a user story including acceptance criteria, acceptance tests, etc.

  • The code has been integrated into the main branch.

Here’s another free online toll that can help you generate random guid generator .You can use the GUID codes to serialize Windows & Mac software or create coupon code for your online store.

Problem Management

Finally, while estimating projects, one of the most important things to remember is to divide down the scoped activities as much as possible. If you have to estimate the height of a building, you probably don’t know where to begin. However, if you figure out how many walls you’ll need to raise and how many bricks you’ll need per square meter, you’ll be in a better negotiating position to precisely draft the project plan.

You have greater control over this now measurable difficulty when work components are smaller. You probably don’t know how to quote a skyscraper, but you must know how much one brick costs.
item.author: davidtz40

item.title: 17 个问题，在很多数据科学面试中被问到 | 17 Questions, that asked in a lot of data science interviews
item.description:
在 SQL 中，您将如何计算中位数？

在 SQL 中，PERCENTILE CONT() 函数用于计算中位数。 此函数有两个参数：要计算中位数的列名和值 0.5。 例如，要计算表中所有员工的工资中位数，请使用以下查询：

选择

来自工人；

  1. 什么是决策树？

决策树是一种预测目标变量值的机器学习模型。 将数据集分成越来越小的子集，直到每个子集只包含一个数据点，从而产生决策树。

  1. 描述您将如何使用决策树来预测客户是否会流失。

要使用决策树来预测客户是否会流失，您必须根据包含先前流失者信息的数据训练模型。 训练模型后，您可以使用它来预测新客户是否会流失。

  1. 什么是梯度提升？

梯度提升是一种机器学习算法，用于提高机器学习模型的准确性。 梯度提升的工作原理是首先训练一系列弱模型，然后结合它们的预测以产生最终预测。

  1. 您如何确保数据的质量？

答：数据科学中的质量保证是一个涉及多个步骤的过程，例如数据清理、数据验证和数据集成。 我使用各种技术来确保数据质量，包括数据可视化、统计分析和机器学习算法。

  1. 您能解释一下您遇到的困难数据分析挑战以及您是如何克服它的吗？

回答：[举例说明你曾面临的困难数据分析挑战以及你是如何克服它的，例如处理大型和复杂的数据集、解决数据完整性问题或处理缺失数据]。 解释你为解决问题所采取的步骤、你用来分析数据的技术以及你取得的结果。

  1. 您如何为给定问题选择模型？

回答：为给定问题选择正确的模型取决于数据的性质和问题的要求。 通常，我首先评估不同类型的模型及其在数据样本上的表现。 然后我使用交叉验证等技术来衡量所选模型的泛化程度。 之后，我用不同的参数进行试验，并使用准确率、精确率和召回率等指标评估模型。

  1. 您能解释一下您创建的特定数据可视化及其提供的见解吗？

答案：[提供您创建的特定数据可视化的示例，例如热图、散点图或条形图，以及它提供的见解]。 解释您正在可视化的数据、用于创建可视化的工具以及您能够从中得出的关键见解。

  1. 您对数据科学过程的了解程度如何？

答：数据科学过程通常包括问题定义、数据收集和清理、数据探索、模型构建和评估以及数据部署等步骤。

  1. 您在数据可视化方面的背景是什么？

答：我有使用各种数据可视化工具（如 Matplotlib、Seaborn 和 Tableau）创建可视化效果的经验，例如折线图、条形图、直方图、散点图和热图。 我认识到数据可视化在获得洞察力和交流结果方面的重要性。

  1. 您在机器学习算法方面的背景是什么？

答：我使用过多种机器学习算法，包括线性和逻辑回归、决策树和随机森林等监督学习技术，以及聚类和降维等非监督学习技术。 我熟悉各种机器学习算法的优点和缺点，以及每种算法的最佳应用。

  1. 应该如何处理数据集中缺失的数据？

回答：有几种处理缺失数据的方法，包括用数据的均值或中值替换缺失值，采用可以处理缺失数据的机器学习算法，例如随机森林，以及删除包含缺失数据的行或列。 最佳方法取决于数据集和缺失数据的性质。

  1. 您能否描述您执行的具体数据分析及其提供的见解？

答案：[解释你完成的具体数据分析及其提供的见解，例如销售数据的时间序列分析或客户人口统计的回归分析]。 解释您正在分析的数据、您使用的技术以及您从分析中获得的关键见解。

  1. 您如何为特定问题选择模型？
    回答：我通常首先选择一些适合手头问题的模型，然后使用准确性和精度等指标评估它们的性能。 然后使用交叉验证和超参数调整等技术优化模型。

  2. 您如何评估模型的性能？
    答：准确率、准确率、召回率和 F1 分数等指标可用于评估模型的性能。 我还使用各种可视化技术来评估模型，例如混淆矩阵和 ROC 曲线。 此外，我还使用交叉验证、k 折和 holdout 方法等技术评估模型的性能。

  3. 应该如何处理数据驱动的问题？
    回答：我处理数据驱动问题的方法是首先理解它，然后收集数据、清理和预处理数据，最后探索数据以找到模式和见解。 之后，我开始构建模型并对其进行微调以提高准确性。 最后，我评估模型并实施解决方案。



原文:

SQL (Structured Query Language) is a programming language for manipulating and querying data in databases. SQL can be used to insert, delete, and update data in a database, as well as retrieve data from it.

  1. In SQL, how would you compute the median?

In SQL, the PERCENTILE CONT() function is used to calculate the median. This function takes two arguments: the column name for which the median is to be calculated and the value 0.5. (which corresponds to the median).

To calculate the median salary for all employees in a table, for example, use the following query:

SELECT

AS median salary PERCENTILE CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY salary)

FROM WORKERS;

  1. What exactly is a decision tree?

A decision tree is a machine-learning model that predicts the value of a target variable. Splitting the data set into smaller and smaller subsets until each subset contains only one data point yields decision trees.

  1. Describe how you would use a decision tree to predict whether or not a customer will churn.

To use a decision tree to predict whether or not a customer will churn, you must train the model on data that includes information about previous churners. After training the model, you can use it to predict whether or not new customers will churn.

  1. What exactly is gradient boosting?

Gradient boosting is a machine learning algorithm used to increase the accuracy of a machine learning model. Gradient boosting works by first training a series of weak models and then combining their predictions to produce a final prediction.

  1. How do you ensure the quality of your data?

Answer: Quality assurance in data science is a process that involves multiple steps, such as data cleaning, data validation, and data integration. I use various techniques to ensure data quality, including data visualization, statistical analysis, and machine learning algorithms.

  1. Can you explain a difficult data analysis challenge you have faced and how you overcame it?

Answer: [Provide an example of a difficult data analysis challenge you have faced and how you overcame it, such as dealing with large and complex datasets, solving data integrity problems, or handling missing data]. Explain the steps you took to address the problem, the techniques you used to analyze the data, and the results you achieved.

  1. How do you select a model for a given problem?

Answer: Choosing the right model for a given problem depends on the nature of the data and the requirements of the problem. Typically, I start by evaluating different types of models and their performance on a sample of the data. Then I use techniques like cross-validation to measure the generalization of the chosen model. After that, I experiment with different parameters and evaluate the model with metrics like accuracy, precision, and recall.

  1. Can you explain a specific data visualization you have created and the insights it provided?

Answer: [Provide an example of a specific data visualization you have created, such as a heatmap, scatter plot, or bar chart, and the insights it provided]. Explain the data you were visualizing, the tools you used to create the visualization, and the key insights you were able to draw from it.

  1. How well do you understand the data science process?

Answer: The data science process typically includes steps such as problem definition, data collection and cleaning, data exploration, model building and evaluation, and data deployment.

  1. What is your background in data visualization?

Answer: I have experience creating visualizations such as line charts, bar charts, histograms, scatter plots, and heatmaps using various data visualization tools such as Matplotlib, Seaborn, and Tableau. I recognize the significance of data visualization in gaining insights and communicating results.

  1. What is your background in machine learning algorithms?

Answer: I've used a variety of machine learning algorithms, including supervised learning techniques like linear and logistic regression, decision trees, and random forests, as well as unsupervised learning techniques like clustering and dimensionality reduction. I am familiar with the advantages and drawbacks of various machine learning algorithms, in addition to the best applications for each.

  1. How should missing data in a dataset be handled?

Answer: There are several approaches to dealing with missing data, including replacing missing values with the mean or median of the data, employing machine learning algorithms that can handle missing data, such as random forests, and dropping the rows or columns containing missing data. The best method depends on the dataset and the nature of the missing data.

  1. Can you describe a specific data analysis you carried out and the insights it provided?

Answer: [Explain a specific data analysis you completed and the insights it provided, such as a time series analysis of sales data or a regression analysis of customer demographics]. Explain the data you were analyzing, the techniques you used, and the key insights you gained from the analysis.

  1. How do you choose a model for a specific problem?
    Answer: I usually start by choosing a few models that are appropriate for the problem at hand and evaluating their performance using metrics like accuracy and precision. The model is then optimized using techniques such as cross-validation and hyperparameter tuning.

  2. How do you assess the performance of a model?
    Answer: Metrics such as accuracy, precision, recall, and F1 score can be used to assess the model's performance. I also use various visualization techniques to evaluate the model, such as the confusion matrix and the ROC Curve. In addition, I evaluate the model's performance using techniques such as cross-validation, k-fold, and the holdout method.

  3. How should a data-driven problem be approached?
    Answer: I approach a data-driven problem by first understanding it, then gathering data, cleaning and pre-processing the data, and finally exploring the data to find patterns and insights. Following that, I begin building the models and fine-tuning them to improve accuracy. Finally, I assess the models and implement the solution.
item.author: gaurav_meherkhamb

item.title: JavaScript 回调 | JavaScript Callbacks
item.description:
回调是用作另一个函数的参数的函数，这样函数就可以调用另一个函数。
听起来有点复杂，不是吗？ 让我们分解一下……

一、了解函数在JavaScript中是如何运行的

在 JavaScript 中，函数根据您调用（告诉它们开始执行）的顺序运行，而不是根据您定义（编写）它们的顺序运行。

定义函数：

 function first() { return “I am first”; } function second() { return “I am second”; }

以相反的顺序调用函数：

哪个会先被调用？

我们称之为 first - second(); 结果，我们将看到输出“我是第二”；

然而，有时有些情况下我们想要控制哪些函数在什么时候运行，并且以特定的顺序调用它们是不够的。 这是为什么它并不总是有用的示例。

选项1

定义函数：

 function displayResults(results) { //some code running document.getElementById(“results”).innerHTML = results; } function googleSomething(keyword) { //some code running return searchResults; }

按所需顺序调用函数：

 Let searchKeyword = googleSomething(“How to JavaScript”); displayResults(searchKeyword);

为什么选项 1 并不总是有用？

因为您将不得不不断调用所有函数，当您有很多函数时，它会变得混乱，您可能最终会得到不需要或忘记的额外函数。 还有其他原因吗？ 在下面评论。

选项 2

定义函数：

 function displayResults(results) { //some code running document.getElementById(“results”).innerHTML = results; } function googleSomething(keyword) { //some code running displayResults(searchResults); }

仅调用搜索功能：

 googleSomething(“How to JavaScript”);

为什么选项 2 并不总是有用？

因为您将无法阻止 googleSomething 函数不显示searchResults ，所以它将始终在其中运行 displayResults 函数。

在这种情况下回调有何帮助？

您可以使用回调函数代替手动编写函数调用或在不需要时运行函数。 您将通过这种方式执行的操作是，在第一个函数准备好返回结果之前，第二个函数（回调函数）不会运行。 简而言之，如果您不运行第一个函数，则第二个函数永远不会运行。

定义函数：

 function displayResults(results) { //some code running document.getElementById(“results”).innerHTML = results; } function googleSomething(keyword, **displayResults**) { //some code running displayResults(searchResults); }

仅调用搜索功能：

 googleSomething(“How to JavaScript”, **displayResults**);

快速说明：当将函数作为回调传递时，您不需要调用它，只需传递名称，不需要括号。

什么时候需要使用回调函数？

首先，您需要了解什么是异步函数。 关于它的内容很多，但我会尽量简单准确地解释它。

他们将需要执行一段可能会花费大量时间的代码，而不是停止一切，他们在后面工作。

让我们举一个愚蠢的例子，比如麦当劳（不是麦当劳的忠实粉丝）——当你点餐时，你继续前进并等待准备。
同时，其他人也在您等待的同时下订单。 队列中的所有人都不会等到你点餐，直到你吃完饭，直到你吃完那顿饭。 为每个客户服务需要花费很多时间，对吗？

这不是一个很好的例子，但我认为它在某种程度上是相似的。 在这种情况下，您的订单准备功能将是异步的。 在人们下单或等待的同时，食物准备功能正在执行中。

那么再一次，这种情况下的回调函数是什么？

回调函数被称为回调是有原因的！ 该功能说，“给我回电话！”。 最后说一下，我们准备食物的异步函数已经执行完毕，可以给你一个结果了。 并且您想在收到此结果之前运行另一个函数，一个将调用您的名字和订单名称的回调函数！

但是您不希望只有在您的食物准备好时才有人叫您的名字，对吗？ 为此，您将把这个“叫我的名字”功能附加到食物准备中。 所以当食物准备功能完成时，它会运行一个调用你名字的回调函数！

简而言之，回调函数是另一个函数的参数，仅在第一个函数执行完毕后才执行。

关于回调还有很多其他有趣的事情，但让我们在这里停下来了解一下基本知识。

这有什么意义吗？ 👀 Please do let me know 👀 请让我知道



原文:

The callback is a function that is used as an argument of another function and this way function is able to call another function.
Sounds a little complicated, doesn’t it? Let’s break it down…

First, understand how functions run in JavaScript

In JavaScript, the functions run according to the order you called (told them to start executing) not according to the order you defined (wrote) them.

Defining function:

function first() {
  return “I am first”;
}

function second() {
  return “I am second”;
}

Calling a function in reversed order:

Which one is going to be called first?

The one we called first - second(); and as a result, we will see the output “I am second”;

However, sometimes there are situations when we want to control which functions run when, and calling them in a specific order is not enough. Here is an example of why it’s not always helpful.

Option 1

Defining function: 

function displayResults(results) {
//some code running
  document.getElementById(“results”).innerHTML = results;
}

function googleSomething(keyword) {
//some code running
  return searchResults;
}

Calling function in the desired order: 

Let searchKeyword = googleSomething(“How to JavaScript”);
displayResults(searchKeyword);

Why Option 1 is not always useful?

Because you will have to constantly call all the functions and when you have a lot of functions it can get messy and you might end up with extra functions you don't need or forget them. Any other reasons? Comment down below.

Option 2

Defining function: 

function displayResults(results) {
//some code running
  document.getElementById(“results”).innerHTML = results;
}

function googleSomething(keyword) {
//some code running
  displayResults(searchResults);
}

Calling only search function: 

googleSomething(“How to JavaScript”);

Why Option 2 is not always useful?

Because you will not be able to prevent the googleSomething function not to display the searchResults, it will always run displayResults function inside it.

How can a callback help in this case?

Instead of writing the function call manually or running the function when you don’t need it, you can use a callback function instead. What you will do this way, is that the second function (the callback function) is not going to run until the first function is ready to return the result. To be short, if you don’t run the first function, the second one will never run.

Defining function: 

function displayResults(results) {
//some code running
  document.getElementById(“results”).innerHTML = results;
}

function googleSomething(keyword, **displayResults**) {
//some code running
  displayResults(searchResults);
}

Calling only search function : 

googleSomething(“How to JavaScript”, **displayResults**);

The second argument displayResults in the googleSomething function is a callback! And a quick note: when passing a function as a callback you don’t need to invoke it, just pass the name, no need for parenthesis.

When do I need to use the callback function?

First, you need to understand what an asynchronous function is. There is a lot about it but I will try to explain it as simply and as accurately as I can.

There are types of functions in JavaScript that are asynchronous - they do not run in sync with the whole code. They will need to execute a code that might take a lot of time and instead of stopping everything, they work in the back.

Let's take a silly example like McDonald's (not a big fan of McDonald's) - when you order a meal you move on and wait for its preparation.
Meanwhile, others also make their orders while you are waiting. All the people in the queue are not going to wait until you order until your meal is completed and until you take that meal. It would take so much time to serve each customer, right?

Not a great example but I think it is something similar to some extent. Your order preparation function will be asynchronous in this case. While people make orders or wait, the food preparation function is in the process of execution.

So once again, what will be the callback function in this case?

The callback function is called callback for a reason! The function says, “call me back!”. Let’s say finally, our asynchronous function of food preparation has finished its execution and is ready to give you a result. And you want to run another function before receiving this result, a callback function that will call your name and the order name!

But you don’t want someone to call your name only when your food is ready, right? 😤

To achieve this, you are going to attach this “call my name” function to the food preparation. So when the food preparation function is finished it will run a callback function that calls your name!

To be short, a callback function is an argument of another function that is executed only when the first one is done executing.

There are a lot of other interesting things about callbacks but let’s stop here just for the basic understanding.

Did it make any sense though? 👀 Please do let me know
item.author: catherineisonline

item.title: 人类心理学如何用于应用程序中的用户体验设计 | How human psychology is used for user experience design in apps
item.description:
克里希纳卡卡德

发表于 1 月 11 日

心理学如何用于应用程序中的用户体验设计👁️

  • 首先，我们将以与毒品有关的 Instagram 帖子为例！

初尝免费

现实生活中的例子

糖被归类为毒品 你在一家糖果店，你向店主要了一颗糖，他会免费给你尝尝，但后来你感觉很好，想要更多

那你得花钱买更多！

现在以 Instagram 为例
你在 Instagram 上发布图片，现在你免费获得了 400 个赞，但过了一段时间你想要超过 1000 个赞，然后 Instagram 要求你以 10 美元或 20 美元的价格推广帖子。

塑造行为
如果你想让你的狗和你一起玩，你必须做一件事，比如如果他踢球，就把球扔给他，或者把球还给你，给他/她饼干，不幸的是他/她没有这样做，那就不要那样做，做一个严师。

例如在我的呼吸中——心理健康 UX 和 UI 案例研究

保持连胜的津贴

保持五周的连胜并获得一次免费治疗，外加两个免费的睡眠故事。

这就是您可以塑造用户行为的方式。

从众效应

就像我们妈妈说的

如果每个人都从桥上跳下去，你也会跳吗？
现在社交媒体上有这么多人，很想加入并在这些平台上花费大量时间。 然而，重要的是要考虑这是否是对我们时间的良好利用，以及它是否对我们有益。

希克定律

希克定律是一项心理学原理，它指出一个人的选择越多，他或她做出最佳选择的决定所需的时间就越长。

现实生活中的例子

在 Nike 陈列室或其他优质商店购物时，选择有限的选项通常会很有帮助。 例如，如果要搜索鞋子，客户可能只能在几个不同的型号之间进行选择，例如 Nike Jordan One 或 Nike Jordan Airmax 运动鞋。 这样可以更轻松地做出决定并更快地购买所需的商品。 通过减少可用选项的数量，简化了决策过程，使客户能够更有效地采取行动。

注意：如果您正在招聘 UX 和 UI 设计Krishna 的投资组合中的职位，请查看我的投资组合

祝您有美好的一天，感谢您的阅读！

一旦被暂停，krishnakakade 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被取消。

解除暂停后，krishnakakade 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，krishnakakade 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 krishnakakade 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保护 DEV 社区 👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 krishnakakade：

降低 krishnakakade 发布的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 krishnakakade 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

krishna kakade

Posted on Jan 11

How psychology is used for UX design in apps👁️

  • First of all we will take the example of Instagram posts in relation with drugs!

The First Taste is free

Real-life example

Sugar is classified as a drug You're in the Sweet shop, You ask the shop owner for one sweet he will give you that for free to taste but later you felt good and you want more

then you have to pay money to buy more!

Now In case of Instagram
You post pictures on Instagram and now you got 400 likes for free but after some time you wanted more likes than 1000 then Instagram asks you to promote posts for 10$ or 20$.

Shape the behavior
If you want your dog to play with you have to do one thing like throw ball at him if he kicks the ball or give it back to you give him/her biscuit unfortunately he/she didn't do it then don't do that, be a strict teacher.

Incase apps you're designing
For example in my Breath - mental health UX & UI Case Study

Perks for maintaining streaks

Maintain a streak for five weeks and get a free therapy session, plus two free sleep stories.

That's how you can shape user's behavior.

Bandwagon Effect

Like our mom says

if everybody jumped off a bridge, would you do it too?
With so many people on social media nowadays, it can be tempting to join in and spend a lot of time on these platforms. However, it's important to consider whether or not this is a good use of our time and whether or not it is beneficial for us.

Hick's law

Hick's law is a psychological principle which states that the more options are available to a person, the longer it will take for him or her to make a decision about which option is best.

Real life Example

When shopping at a Nike showroom or other quality store, it is often helpful to have a limited selection of options to choose from. For example, if searching for shoes, a customer may only have the choice between a few different models such as Nike Jordan One or Nike Jordan Airmax sneakers. This can make it easier to make a decision and purchase the desired item more quickly. By having a smaller number of options available, the decision-making process is streamlined, allowing customers to act more efficiently.

Note : Please take a look at my portfolio if you are hiring for positions in UX and UI Design Krishna's Portfolio

Wishing you a great day ahead, and thank you for reading!

Matheus Maximiliano - May 15 '22

item.title: 用于 React Native 的 CICD - Android 和 iOS | CICD for React Native - Android and iOS
item.description:
发表于 1 月 11 日

在创建 React Native 应用程序时，很快就会发现每次进行更改时生成 APK 变得非常乏味。 再加上您需要在 Xcode 上存档构建并分发到 Appstore，以便在 Testflight 或 Appstore 上发布。

我在两个平台上使用了两种不同的自动部署方法，但都在推送到特定分支时触发。

在开发中，我使用dev分支作为活动分支，所有更改都合并到

  • 对于 Android，我决定使用GitHub Actions ，然后将应用程序推送到Firebase App Distribution ，并通过电子邮件向测试人员发送通知
  • 对于 iOS， XCode Cloud为我完成了自动发布到 Testflight 并通过推送通知和电子邮件向用户发送通知的工作

让我们开始吧

在项目的根目录下创建一个.github/workflows文件夹
  • 在.github/workflows中创建一个build.yml文件

    1. 添加下面的代码。 非常自我解释，名称解释了每个动作
    name: Build And Deploy App To Firebase Distribution on: push: branches: - dev jobs: build: runs-on: ubuntu-latest steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v3 - name: set up JDK 1.8 uses: actions/setup-java@v3 with: distribution: temurin java-version: 11 - name: Setup Android SDK uses: android-actions/setup-android@v2 - name: Setup Gradle uses: gradle/gradle-build-action@v2 - name: Get yarn cache directory path id: yarn-cache-dir-path run: echo "::set-output name=dir::$(yarn cache dir)" - name: Restore node_modules from cache uses: actions/cache@v2 id: yarn-cache with: path: ${{ steps.yarn-cache-dir-path.outputs.dir }} key: ${{ runner.os }}-yarn-${{ hashFiles('**/yarn.lock') }} restore-keys: | ${{ runner.os }}-yarn- - name: Install dependencies run: yarn install - name: Make Gradlew Executable run: cd android && chmod +x ./gradlew - name: build release run: cd android && ./gradlew clean && ./gradlew assembleRelease --no-daemon - name: Upload artifact to Firebase App Distribution uses: wzieba/Firebase-Distribution-Github-Action@v1 with: appId: ${{secrets.FIREBASE_APP_ID}} token: ${{secrets.FIREBASE_TOKEN}} groups: test-group file: android/app/build/outputs/apk/release/app-universal-release.apk
    1. 在设置 Firebase 应用程序分发时，您需要指定从 Firebase 获取的 FIREBASE_APP_ID 和 FIREBASE_TOKEN

    1. 完成并推送后，CICD 应该启动。可以在“ Actions ”选项卡下访问进度和结果。

    一些调试技巧

    • 尝试先在本地运行cd android && ./gradlew clean && ./gradlew assembleRelease并确保它成功
    • 如果构建失败，GitHub Action 事件将指出错误，以便您调试和修复

    在项目的/ios文件夹中创建一个.ci_scripts文件夹
  • 在.github/workflows中创建一个ci_post_clone.sh文件

    1. 将此代码粘贴到ci_post_clone.sh文件中
    #!/bin/sh export HOMEBREW_NO_INSTALL_CLEANUP=TRUE brew install cocoapods # have to add node yourself brew install node@16 # link it to the path brew link node@16 brew install yarn # Install dependencies you manage with CocoaPods. yarn pod install # the sed command from RN cant find the file... so we have to run it ourselves sed -i -e $'s/ && (__IPHONE_OS_VERSION_MIN_REQUIRED < __IPHONE_10_0)//' /Volumes/workspace/repository/ios/Pods/RCT-Folly/folly/portability/Time.h

    可选：将此添加到您的Info.plist文件以解决在将应用程序发布到 Testflight 之前所需的加密对话框检查

    <key>ITSAppUsesNonExemptEncryption</key> <false/>

    1. 推送到要用于部署的 github 分支。 就我而言dev

    2. 启动 Xcode 并转到Product并选择Xcode Cloud

    3. 按照提示将 Xcode 云连接到您的 GitHub 项目，指定dev分支或您正在处理的任何分支

    4. 剩下的工作将在 Appstore connect 接口上完成。 稍后将使用这些附加步骤更新本文

    如果您有任何问题，请告诉我。

    中提琴

    道格拉斯·马查多 - 22 年 12 月 16 日

    一旦暂停，suulola 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

    解除暂停后，suulola 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

    取消发布后，suulola 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

    如果 suulola 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

    笔记：

    感谢您保护 DEV 社区 👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 suulola 的方法：

    降低 suulola 的所有帖子的可见度

    报告其他不当行为

    不屈不挠的 suulola 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



    原文:

    Suulola Oluwaseyi

    Posted on Jan 11

    When creating a React Native application, it soon becomes evident that generating an APK each time a change is made becomes really tedious. Coupled with the fact that you need to Archive the build on Xcode and Distribute to Appstore for either publishing on Testflight or Appstore.

    I used two different approaches for auto deployment on both platforms but both are trigger on pushing to a particular branch.

    While in development, I use the dev branch as the active branch all changes are merged into

    • For Android, I decided to go with GitHub Actions that then pushes the app to Firebase App Distribution and notification sent out to the testers via email
    • For iOS, XCode Cloud did the job for me which is published to Testflight automatically and notification sent out to the users via push notification and email

    Let's get right in

    Android using GitHub Action and Firebase App Distribution

    1. Create a .github/workflows folder in the root of the project
    2. Create a build.yml file inside the .github/workflows

    1. Add the code below. Pretty self explanatory and the name explains each action 
    name: Build And Deploy App To Firebase Distribution

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - dev

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v3
      - name: set up JDK 1.8
        uses: actions/setup-java@v3
        with:
          distribution: temurin
          java-version: 11
      - name: Setup Android SDK
        uses: android-actions/setup-android@v2
      - name: Setup Gradle
        uses: gradle/gradle-build-action@v2
      - name: Get yarn cache directory path
        id: yarn-cache-dir-path
        run: echo "::set-output name=dir::$(yarn cache dir)"
      - name: Restore node_modules from cache
        uses: actions/cache@v2
        id: yarn-cache
        with:
          path: ${{ steps.yarn-cache-dir-path.outputs.dir }}
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-yarn-${{ hashFiles('**/yarn.lock') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-yarn-
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: yarn install
      - name: Make Gradlew Executable
        run: cd android && chmod +x ./gradlew
      - name: build release
        run: cd android && ./gradlew clean && ./gradlew assembleRelease --no-daemon
      - name: Upload artifact to Firebase App Distribution
        uses: wzieba/Firebase-Distribution-Github-Action@v1
        with:
          appId: ${{secrets.FIREBASE_APP_ID}}
          token: ${{secrets.FIREBASE_TOKEN}}
          groups: test-group
          file: android/app/build/outputs/apk/release/app-universal-release.apk
    1. You'll need to specify the FIREBASE_APP_ID and FIREBASE_TOKEN gotten from Firebase when setting up Firebase App distribution

    1. Once that is done and you push, the CICD should kick in. The progress and result can be accessed under the Actions tab.

    Some debugging tips

    • try to run cd android && ./gradlew clean && ./gradlew assembleRelease locally first and ensure it's successful
    • The GitHub Action event will point out what is wrong if the build fails for you to debug and fix

    iOS using XCode and Testflight

    1. Create a .ci_scripts folder in /ios folder of your project
    2. Create a ci_post_clone.sh file inside the .github/workflows

    1. Paste this code in ci_post_clone.sh file 
    #!/bin/sh

export HOMEBREW_NO_INSTALL_CLEANUP=TRUE
brew install cocoapods
# have to add node yourself
brew install node@16
# link it to the path
brew link node@16

brew install yarn

# Install dependencies you manage with CocoaPods.
yarn
pod install
# the sed command from RN cant find the file... so we have to run it ourselves
sed -i -e  $'s/ && (__IPHONE_OS_VERSION_MIN_REQUIRED < __IPHONE_10_0)//' /Volumes/workspace/repository/ios/Pods/RCT-Folly/folly/portability/Time.h

    OPTIONAL: Add this to your Info.plist file to resolve the encryption dialog check required before publishing an app to Testflight

    <key>ITSAppUsesNonExemptEncryption</key>
<false/>

    1. Push to the github branch you want to use for deployment. In my case dev

    2. Launch Xcode and go to Product and Select Xcode Cloud

    3. Follow the prompt and connect Xcode cloud to your GitHub project, specifying the dev branch or whatever branch you are working on

    4. The remaining work will be done on the Appstore connect interface. Will update this article with those additional steps later on

    Let me know if you have any questions.

    Viola

    Douglas Machado - Dec 16 '22

    Vincent Tsen - Dec 16 '22

    Once suspended, suulola will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

    Once unsuspended, suulola will be able to comment and publish posts again.

    Once unpublished, all posts by suulola will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

    If suulola is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

    Note:

    Thanks for keeping DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻 safe. Here is what you can do to flag suulola:

    Report other inappropriate conduct

    Unflagging suulola will restore default visibility to their posts.

item.title: Apple Watch Series 7 铝金属 | Apple Watch Series 7 Aluminum
item.description:
梅尔文里奥斯

发表于 1 月 11 日

这款设备纤薄、轻巧且设计精美，可满足所有需求。 查看短信和电子邮件，在手腕上接听电话并为会议计时，通过跟踪您的步数和锻炼保持活跃，使用 Apple Pay 随时随地购买东西，让 Siri 帮助您完成更多工作。

item.title: Una pequeña 介绍 Matplotlib 的子图 | Una pequeña intro a las subplots de Matplotlib
item.description:
吉列尔莫

发表于 1 月 11 日

一旦暂停，omem 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

解除暂停后，omem 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，omem 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 omem 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保护 DEV 社区 👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 omem 的方法：

降低 omem 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 omem 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Guillermo

Posted on Jan 11

En este muy pequeño post nos centraremos en la tarea de crear varios gráficos en una sola imagen con la librería Matplotlib.

La manera más fácil de lograr nuestra tarea es usando matplotlib.pyplot.subplots. Este método retorna una tupla (fig, ax), donde fig hace referencia a Figure el cual es la imagen contenedora de todos los sub-gráficos, y ax hace referencia a un arreglo de Axes, estos se refieren a cada sub-gráfico de manera individual.

Al método anterior le podemos especificar el número de filas y columnas para generar tantos sub-gráficos como nosotros consideremos necesario. Para entender esto de mejor manera pasemos al código:

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
plt.style.use('ggplot')
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(12,4) ,nrows=1, ncols=3)

Ahora veamos como graficar en los espacios generados, para esto crearemos unos datos con ayuda de numpy.

import numpy as np
x = np.linspace(0, 2 * np.pi, 400)
y1 = np.cos(x**2)
y2 = np.sin(x**2)
y3 = np.floor(x**2)

Ahora sí grafiquemos estas tres funciones:

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(16,4) ,nrows=1, ncols=3)
ax[0].plot(x, y1, color='#581845', linestyle='dashed')
ax[0].set_title('Función coseno')
ax[1].plot(x, y2, color='#0C0C0E', linestyle='dashdot')
ax[1].set_title('Función seno')
ax[2].plot(x, y3, color='#D42D2D')
ax[2].set_title('Función piso')
plt.show()

Alternativamente podemos desempacar los Axes como una tupla:

fig, (ax1, ax2, ax3) = plt.subplots(figsize=(16,4) ,nrows=1, ncols=3)
ax1.plot(x, y1, color='#581845', linestyle='dashed')
ax1.set_title('Función coseno')
ax2.plot(x, y2, color='#0C0C0E', linestyle='dashdot')
ax2.set_title('Función seno')
ax3.plot(x, y3, color='#D42D2D')
ax3.set_title('Función piso')
plt.show()

Y el resultado será el mismo. Aquí ya es una cuestión de gustos el cómo manejar a los Axes.

Ahora generemos un arreglo bidimensional de sub-gráficos y veamos que podemos acceder a cada uno de ellos con índices, como si se tratara de una matriz.

Pero antes de graficar necesitamos crear una cuarta función para poder llenar el último espacio, así que creemos dicha función:

Ahora si procedamos a hacer nuestras gráficas:

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(16,8) ,nrows=2, ncols=2)
ax[0, 0].plot(x, y1, color='#581845', linestyle='dashed')
ax[0, 0].set_title('Función coseno')
ax[0, 1].plot(x, y2, color='#0C0C0E', linestyle='dashdot')
ax[0, 1].set_title('Función seno')
ax[1, 0].plot(x, y3, color='#D42D2D')
ax[1, 0].set_title('Función piso')
ax[1, 1].plot(x, y4, color='#0B13BE')
ax[1, 1].set_title('Función exponencial')
plt.show()

Este ejemplo es muy sencillo y todas las funciones las generamos a partir del mismo dominio (eje X). Sin embargo, puede que también en un caso que estemos estudiando los datos compartan algún eje, por lo que resultaría útil que nuestra imagen no muestre el eje dos veces sino que simplemente se comparta. Lo anterior lo podemos lograr con sharex o sharey.

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(16,8) ,nrows=2, ncols=2, sharex=True)
ax[0, 0].plot(x, y1, color='#581845', linestyle='dashed')
ax[0, 0].set_title('Función coseno')
ax[0, 1].plot(x, y2, color='#0C0C0E', linestyle='dashdot')
ax[0, 1].set_title('Función seno')
ax[1, 0].plot(x, y3, color='#D42D2D')
ax[1, 0].set_title('Función piso')
ax[1, 1].plot(x, y4, color='#0B13BE')
ax[1, 1].set_title('Función exponencial')
plt.show()

Muchas más cosas se pueden personalizar con la finalidad de realizar la visualización que nosotros queramos, pero la idea era hacer este post muy conciso y corto, ya trataremos con mayor profundidad este y otros temas en próximas entradas.

Mil gracias por leer, excelente día :D

Hari Narayan G - Dec 1 '22

item.title: 2023 年开始游戏开发的顶级在线资源 | Top Online Resources To Get Started With Game Development In 2023
item.description:
如果您被游戏开发行业所吸引，那么您一定正在寻找一些最有效的资源来开始学习游戏开发。 在这里，我已经招募了资源来开始你的旅程。 对于初学者、中级学习者和专家，这些可以付费也可以免费使用。

在线游戏开发课程

选择最好的在线游戏开发课程，获得可帮助您脱颖而出的技能。 您可以在 edX、Coursera、Udemy 等平台上找到免费和付费课程，专家和专业人士根据他们在该领域工作多年积累的实践经验教授技术和概念知识。

新手营

游戏新手训练营不仅有助于提升您的技能，还有助于提升您的专业组合，并让您的简历大放异彩。 确保您选择的游戏开发训练营提供可靠的学习成果、优秀的校友评论和广泛的课程。

游戏社区

加入充满热情的游戏开发者社区对掌握游戏开发技能非常有帮助。 这是因为，在这样的空间中，您会发现更多技术娴熟、经验丰富的设计师和开发人员，他们不断分享知识、技巧、建议和有效资源，以精通游戏开发领域。

游戏开发者

另一个致力于游戏开发的网站。 它提供教程、新闻、文章和问答论坛，以帮助有抱负的游戏开发人员。 学习者可以探索热门游戏，甚至可以与其他开发人员合作开展爱好项目，以提高他们的动手能力。

游戏设计资源

您可以访问德克萨斯大学以网站、播客和其他信息资源的形式提供的大量游戏资源。 他们将证明对寻求在游戏设计领域建立职业生涯的学习者非常有帮助。 那是因为您还将探索游戏引擎、免费游戏设计资源、声音软件等。

统一学习

这是全球无数顶级游戏开发机构部署的通用游戏引擎。 无论您在游戏开发之旅中处于哪个级别，培训都会对您的技能组合有所帮助。 此外，Unity Learn Platform 还提供针对性强的学习体验，帮助学生掌握正确的技能，从而在实时 3-D 技术领域和游戏领域开启成功的职业生涯。

游戏分析博客

这个令人惊叹的网站将为您提供有抱负的游戏开发人员制作成功游戏所需的信息。 从游戏开发技巧和营销技巧到成功案例等等，您会发现一切可以帮助您积累知识的内容。 学习者可以浏览内容丰富的博客文章、新闻文章、趋势报告、观看视频等。 因此，如果您希望获得最新的游戏开发培训，请不要跳过此资源。

游戏开发专家在各种 YouTube 频道上提供了精彩的教程。 布拉克尼的频道是 YouTube 上最负盛名和最有用的资源之一。 从 Unity 和设计到编程等等，您会发现很多东西可以提高您的知识和技能。 图书

通过阅读与游戏开发相关的书籍来磨练您的知识。 一些可以指导你的最好的书是：

  • 游戏设计的艺术：杰西·谢尔 (Jesse Schell) 的镜头之书

  • 升级！ 斯科特·罗杰斯 (Scott Rogers) 的《伟大的视频游戏设计指南》

  • 通过 Michael Dawson 的游戏编程开始 C++

  • 因此，这些是一些非常有用的资源，可帮助您开始游戏开发之旅。 请记住，要精通任何领域，都需要坚持不懈和不断练习。

    如果您正在寻找游戏开发工作室来构建您的游戏/应用程序，请来领先的应用程序和游戏开发公司KrewGames 。 他们的灵巧和经验丰富会将您的想法变成引人注目的现实。

    如有任何疑问或想讨论您的绝妙想法，请立即联系KrewGames 业务团队！



原文:

If you’re drawn in by the game development industry, then you must be looking for some of the most effective resources to start learning game development. Here, I have enlisted resources to begin your journey. These can be paid as well as free for beginners, intermediate learners, and experts.

Online game development courses

Choose the best online game development courses to gain skills that help you stand out from the crowd. You can find free as well as paid courses on platforms like edX, Coursera, Udemy, etc., where experts and professionals teach technical and conceptual stuff from their hands-on experience in the field accumulated over years of working in the industry.

Bootcamps

Gaming boot camps are helpful not only in levelling up your skills but your professional portfolio as well to shine your resume. Make sure the game development Bootcamp you choose offers surefire learner outcomes, great alumni reviews, and an extensive course curriculum.

Gaming Community

Entering a community of passionate game developers is immensely helpful in picking up game development skills. That’s because, in such spaces, you’ll find more skilled and experienced designers and developers who continuously share knowledge, tips, advice, and effective resources to gain mastery in the game development arena.

Gamedev

Another website dedicated to game development. It features tutorials, news, articles, and Q&A forums to help aspiring game developers. Learners can explore trending games or even collaborate with other developers on hobby projects to level up their hands-on skills.

Game Design Resources

You can access a great range of gaming resources offered by the University of Texas in the form of websites, podcasts, and other informative resources. They would prove immensely helpful for learners seeking to build their careers in the game design sector. That’s because you will also get to explore game engines, free game design resources, sound software, and more.

Unity Learn

This is an all-purpose game engine deployed by countless top game development agencies across the globe. Unity’s learning space offers in-depth training of over 750 hours with live and on-demand learning resources. No matter what level you’re on in your game development journey, the training will prove helpful to your skillset. Additionally, the Unity Learn Platform also offers well-directed learning experiences that help students to acquire the right skills for initiating a successful career in the real-time 3-D tech field and gaming arena.

Game Analytics Blog

This amazing website will give you the information an aspiring game developer needs to craft successful games. From game development tips and marketing tips to success stories, and more you will find everything to build up your knowledge. Learners can go through informative blog posts, news articles, trend reports, watch videos, and so on. So, if you wish to gain up-to-date training in game development, don’t skip this resource.

YouTube Tutorials

There are amazing tutorials delivered on various YouTube channels by game development experts. Brackney's channel is one of the most prestigious and helpful resources on YouTube. From Unity & design to programming, and more, you will find a lot to sharpen your knowledge and skills. Brackneys releases a new video every Sunday explaining the gaming code while he’s scribing it.

Books

Hone in your knowledge by reading game development-related books. Some of the best books that can guide you are:

  • The Art of Game Design: A Book of Lenses by Jesse Schell

  • Level Up! The Guide to Great Video Game Design by Scott Rogers

  • Game Engine Architecture by Jason Gregory

  • Beginning C++ Through Game Programming by Michael Dawson

  • Game Programming Patterns by Robert Nystrom

So, these were some great and helpful resources to help you begin your game development journey. Remember it takes consistency and practice to acquire mastery over any field.

If you are looking for a game development studio to build your game/ app, come to KrewGames, a leading app and game development company. Their deft and experienced will turn your ideas into a striking reality.

Have any queries or want to discuss your awesome idea, connect with the KrewGames business team today!

item.title: 到 2030 年，流体动力设备市场预计将达到 632.661 亿美元，预测期内复合年增长率为 5.5% | Fluid Power Equipment Market is estimated to be US$ 63266.1 million by 2030 with a CAGR of 5.5% during the forecast period
item.description:
亚达瓦玛09

发表于 1 月 11 日

流体动力设备市场在 2020 年占 378.942 亿美元，预计到 2030 年将达到 632.661 亿美元，预计复合年增长率为 5.5%。 流体动力设备是利用流体产生、控制和传递动力的装置。 流体动力术语用于描述液压和气动技术，其中这两种技术都用作液体或气体等流体，以将动力从一个位置传输到另一个位置。 此外，液压流体通常是油，但也可以是水，而气动流体使用的气体通常是压缩空气。 流体动力设备市场可用于工业包装、物料搬运、机器人技术、采矿活动、建筑和自动化过程。 此外，电力设备还可以在低速下产生高扭矩，因此有助于在预测期内实现市场的显着增长和巨大的收益。 报告“全球流体动力设备市场，按类型（液压和气动，气动液压和气动），按应用（汽车、农业机械、食品加工、石油和天然气机械、医疗设备、包装机械、物料搬运和半导体） )，以及按地区（北美、欧洲、亚太地区、拉丁美洲以及中东和非洲）- 到 2030 年的市场趋势、分析和预测”主要亮点： • 2021 年，Enovation Controls 是 Helios Technologies 的一部分推出全新uControl移动机械控制器，为超恶劣环境下的重载设备提供精确的比例控制，uControl移动机械控制器配备多个CAN通道，具有极高的耐用性，实现整机控制，具有精确的流体动力和设备性能. • Bosch Rexroth 于 2020 年 3 月推出了新的负载敏感阀 RM10（左）和 RM15（右），它们是通用的紧凑型，并且多用途负载敏感方向控制阀，针对移动应用市场进行了优化设计。 为此，该交易预计将于 2020 年底完成。 分析师观点：流体动力设备在工业危险环境中的有效安全是主要驱动因素。 它提供高功率重量比，这将有助于适合采矿厂使用的设备。 此外，流体动力设备可以实现平稳运行，因为它可以在低速下产生高扭矩。 驱动因素是开发节能解决方案的投资快速增长。 此外，技术的进步以及通过开发机器提高流体动力设备的制造率预计将推动预测期内的市场增长。 然而，流体动力设备的安装成本高，预计将阻碍市场。 在购买此报告之前，请点击以下链接索取样品或进行查询：

报告中的主要市场洞察：2020 年全球流体动力设备市场规模为 378.942 亿美元，预计到 2030 年将达到 632.661 亿美元，预计复合年增长率为 5.5%。 全球流体动力设备市场按类型、应用和地区划分。 竞争格局：全球流体动力设备市场的主要运营商是 Alfa Laval AB、Colfax Corporation、Crane、Flowserve Corporation、Graco、Christian Burkert GmbH & Co. KG、Dover Corporation。

该市场提供有关工业基础、生产力、实力、制造商和近期趋势的详细信息，这将有助于公司扩大业务并促进财务增长。 此外，该报告还展示了动态因素，包括细分市场、子细分市场、区域市场、竞争、主要参与者和市场预测。 此外，市场包括最近的合作、合并、收购和合作伙伴关系，以及影响市场轨迹的不同地区的监管框架。 报告中包含影响全球市场的最新技术进步和创新。

其他相关报告：-
永恒的开发帖子...

我多年前就希望知道的 Git 概念

开发人员使用最多的技术不是 Javascript。

它不是 Python 或 HTML。

它甚至很少在面试中被提及或被列为工作的先决条件。

当然，我说的是 Git 和版本控制。

Fluid power equipment is a device in which fluid is used to generate, control and transmit power. Fluid power term is use to describe Hydraulic and pneumatic technologies, in which both technologies are used as a fluid such as liquid or gas to transmit power from one location to another. Moreover, hydraulics fluid is usually oil but can be water, whereas pneumatics uses a gas that is usually compressed air. The fluid power equipment market can be used in industrial packaging, material handling, robotics, mining activities, construction, and automation processes. In addition, electric power equipment can also produce high torque at low speed, thus it helps to have significant growth of the market with great benefits during the forecast period. item.title: 优化 JBoss EAP 7.x 性能的 6 个最佳实践 | 6 Best Practices for Optimizing JBoss EAP 7.x Performance
item.description:
它是基于订阅/开源的，可以在任何支持 Java 的操作系统上使用。 最新的稳定版本是 JBoss EAP 7.x，这里是我们的 JBoss 专家发誓要从您的部署中获得最大投资回报率的 6 大秘诀。

  1. 设置固定的堆大小 最佳应用程序服务器内存设置取决于部署的应用程序、用户数量、安装所在的虚拟或物理主机以及主机上运行的服务。

建议将堆大小设置为最小 4GB 和最大 RAM 的一半，因为超过该值可能会导致性能问题。 服务器在此决策中也起着至关重要的作用。 例如，如果服务器包含 64GB 的 RAM，您可以将最小 Xms 设置为 4GB，将最大 Xms 设置为 32GB。

  1. 配置日志轮换 您可以设置定期轮换文件处理程序创建的备份数。 此处理程序是大小旋转文件处理程序和周期性旋转文件处理程序的组合。 当同时被（尺寸和周期）轮换时，限制只在尺寸部分，在当前时间段内，因为周期优先，尺寸应用于周期。 一旦轮换到不同的日期，它就会忽略之前的日期，并为新的周期设置一个新的限制。 当达到预设日志文件大小时或在预设时间之后，周期性大小轮换文件处理程序将轮换。

时间由使用的后缀决定，例如“.yyyy-MM-dd”每天轮换，“.yyyy-MM-dd_HH:mm”每分钟轮换。 后缀字符串应采用 java.text.SimpleDateFormat 可以理解的格式

  1. 使用 JAVA 堆转储进行故障排除 Java 转储是在特定时间点创建的 JVM 本身的快照。 生成和分析转储文件可用于诊断和解决 Java 应用程序中的问题。 根据使用的 JDK，有不同的方法来为 JBoss EAP 进程生成和解析 Java 存储库。 您可以使用以下命令为 JBoss EAP 创建按需转储：

此命令创建 HPROF 格式的堆转储文件，该文件通常位于 EAP_HOME 或 EAP_HOME/bin.jcmd JAVA_PID GC.heap_dump -all=true FILENAME.hprof

  1. 使用 AggressiveOpts JVM 选项启用积极优化可以为您的环境提供巨大的性能改进。 此设置启用 Java 性能优化功能，这些功能在未来的 Java 版本中应该是标准的。 要启用 AggressiveOpts，请使用以下 JVM 配置：

选择合适的垃圾收集器
对于在 OpenJDK 或 Oracle JDK 上运行的独立 JBoss EAP 服务器，默认启用垃圾收集日志。 我们推荐使用 G1 垃圾收集器，这应该是从 Java 9 开始的标准。G1 垃圾收集器在大多数情况下通常优于 CMS 和并行垃圾收集器。

  • 配置最小和最大池大小
    如果最小池大小太小，这会增加执行第一个数据库命令时的延迟，因为可能需要建立新连接。 如果最小池大小太大，将出现与数据源或资源适配器的不必要的连接。 这是一个重要的性能设置，因为它限制了活动连接的数量，从而限制了并发活动的数量。 因此，需要根据您环境的独特需求仔细配置最小和最大池大小。

    • 本文首发于本公司博客

    原文:

    The JBoss Enterprise Application Platform (JBoss EAP) is a reliable and stable Java EE-based application server runtime platform from Red Hat. It is subscription-based/open-source, and usable on any operating system that supports Java. JBoss EAP is part of Red Hat’s Enterprise Middleware portfolio and is perfect for building, deploying, and hosting highly-transactional Java applications and services.

    The latest stable versions are JBoss EAP 7.x and here are the top 6 secrets our JBoss experts swear by to get the maximum ROI from your deployment.

    1. Setting a fixed Heap size Optimal application server memory settings depend on the applications deployed, the number of users, the virtual or physical host on which the installation resides, and the services running on the host.

    It is recommended to set the Heap size to a minimum of 4GB and a maximum of half of your RAM since beyond that it can lead to performance issues. The server also plays a crucial role in this decision. For example, if the server contains 64GB of RAM, you can set the minimum Xms to 4GB and the maximum Xms to 32 GB.

    1. Configuring log rotation You can set the number of backups that the periodic rotating file handler creates. This handler is a combination of the size-rotating-file-handler and the periodic-rotating-file-handler. When being rotated by both (size and period), the limit is only on the size part, within the current time period, as period takes precedence and the size is applied to the period. Once it rotates to a different date, it ignores the previous ones and has a new limit for the new period. The Periodic Size Rotating File Handler will rotate when the pre-set log file size is reached or after a pre-set time.

    The time is determined by the suffix used, for example, “.yyyy-MM-dd” would rotate every day and “.yyyy-MM-dd_HH:mm” would rotate every minute. The suffix string should be in a format which can be understood by java.text.SimpleDateFormat

    1. Troubleshooting with JAVA Heap dump A Java dump is a snapshot of the JVM itself, created at a specific point in time. Generating and analysing dump files can be useful for diagnosing and troubleshooting problems in Java applications. Depending on which JDK is in use, there are different ways to generate and parse a Java repository for the JBoss EAP process. You can use the following command to create an on-demand dump for JBoss EAP:

    jcmd JAVA_PID GC.heap_dump -all=true FILENAME.hprof

    This command creates a heap dump file in the HPROF format, which is usually located in EAP_HOME or EAP_HOME/bin.jcmd JAVA_PID GC.heap_dump -all=true FILENAME.hprof

    1. Enabling Aggressive Optimizations Using the AggressiveOpts JVM option can provide massive performance improvements to your environment. This setting enables Java performance optimization features that should be standard in future versions of Java. To enable AggressiveOpts, use the following JVM configuration:

    XX:+AggressiveOpts

    1. Choosing the right Garbage Collector
      For standalone JBoss EAP servers running on OpenJDK or Oracle JDK, garbage collection logging is enabled by default. We recommend using the G1 garbage collector, which should be the standard starting with Java 9. The G1 garbage collector is generally superior to CMS and parallel garbage collectors in most cases.

    2. Configuring Min and Max Pool Size
      The min-pool-size property specifies the minimum connection pool size. If the minimum pool size is too small, this can increase latency when the first database command is executed, as new connections may need to be established. If the minimum pool size is too large, unnecessary connections to the data source or resource adapter will occur. The max-pool-size attribute specifies the maximum connection pool size. This is an important performance setting because it limits the number of active connections and thus the amount of concurrent activity. So, minimum and maximum pool sizes need to be carefully configured based on the unique needs of your environment.

    This article was originally published on our company blog

item.title: 如何确保 Salesforce Classic 到 Lightning 的成功迁移 | How to ensure successful Salesforce Classic to Lightning Migration
item.description:
多年来，Salesforce 已经从传统的 CRM 发展成为企业应用程序开发平台。 您现在可以使用 Salesforce 为 CPQ、履行、计费等创建自定义企业应用程序。 但是，为此，您需要从 Classic 迁移到 Lightning。

它不仅承诺更快的导航和更直观的用户体验，而且还允许业务用户和开发人员使用其低代码开发框架创建自定义应用程序。 尽管从 Classic 到 Lightning 的好处数不胜数，但企业领导者对迁移犹豫不决。

从 Classic 迁移到 Lightning 可能是一项艰巨的任务。 它需要大量的时间、精力，而且容易出现风险。 在本文中，我们将重点介绍确保 Salesforce Classic 成功迁移到 Lightning 的最佳实践。

准备情况检查是迁移的第一步，因为它允许您识别当前 Classic 和 Lightning 之间的差距。 它还可以帮助您了解什么会受到影响，需要多少时间和精力才能进行切换。 通过执行准备情况检查，您将能够了解潜在问题并确定您当前正在使用但将不再在 Lightning 中使用的功能。

缺口分析
一旦您了解了您的团队如何通过就绪检查来使用 Classic，就该执行差距分析了。 并非 Classic 中的所有功能都可在 Lightning 中使用。 因此，您需要确定可以填补空白的 Lightning 功能。 您还需要确定将改进流程和系统的功能。

进行迁移
一些企业选择一次性迁移到 Lightning。 由于 Salesforce 是一个大型应用程序，涉及从 CRM 到 CPQ 到履行到计费等等的方方面面，如果您逐步进行迁移，一个团队一个团队地迁移，那将是一件好事。 企业越大，逐步铺开越好。

测试您的迁移
在沙盒测试环境中测试 Lightning 非常重要，因为一些现有的 Classic 功能已被 Lightning 功能取代。 此外，测试还可以确保定制和集成按预期工作。 测试还涵盖 Lightning Experience 就绪检查期间未涵盖的功能。 需要执行详尽的用户验收测试和功能测试，以了解每个更改的影响。

迁移后测试
每当推出季节性版本时，都需要测试每个定制和集成。 因此，测试不能被称为一次性任务，而是一个持续的过程。

由于在迁移和上线后需要进行多轮测试，因此手动执行此操作不能被视为可行的解决方案。 您所需要的只是合并自动化 Salesforce 测试。 然而，所有可用的测试自动化工具都不会起作用，因为 Salesforce 迁移的主要利益相关者是业务用户。 他们是带宽有限的非技术人员。 因此，您不能选择基于代码的测试自动化平台。

无代码 Salesforce 测试平台正是您所需要的。

您需要引入一个学习曲线低的无代码 Salesforce 测试平台。 借助低代码测试自动化平台，业务用户可以积极参与测试并轻松验证业务流程。
还要在测试自动化平台中寻找自我修复功能，因为 Salesforce 是一个动态应用程序，并且每当推出季节性版本时，测试自动化脚本通常会变得脆弱。

雷切尔索德伯格 - 20 年 7 月 14 日

雷切尔索德伯格 - 20 年 6 月 11 日

原文:


Over the years, Salesforce has grown from a traditional CRM to enterprise app development platform. You can now create custom enterprise apps for CPQ, fulfillment, billing and more with Salesforce. However, for this, you need to migrate from Classic to Lightning.

Lightning is a modern interface that is built on top of Salesforce’s mobile application technology. Not only it promises faster navigation and a more intuitive user experience but also allows business users and developers to create custom apps with its low-code development framework. Although the benefits of Classic to Lightning are countless, business leaders hesitate to migrate.

Migration from Classic to Lightning can be a daunting task. It requires a lot of time, effort, and is prone to risks. In this article, we’ll highlight best practices to ensure successful Salesforce Classic to Lightning migration.

‍‍‍Critical Salesforce Classic to Lightning migration steps Lightning readiness check

Readiness Check is the first step towards migration as it allows you to identify gaps between your current Classic and Lightning. It also helps you to understand what will be impacted, how much time and effort will be required to switch over. By performing readiness checks, you’ll be able to understand potential issues and identify features which you’re using currently but will no longer be available in Lightning.

Gap analysis
Once you understand how your team uses Classic using readiness checks, it’s time to perform gap analysis. Not all features from Classic are available in Lightning. So, you need to identify Lightning features that will fill the gap. You also need to identify features that will improve the processes and systems.

Undergo the migration
Some enterprises opt to migrate to Lightning in one go. Since Salesforce is a big application that touches everything from CRM to CPQ to fulfillment to billing and more, it would be good if you undergo migration gradually, migrating team by team. The bigger the enterprise, the better it is to roll out gradually.

Test your migration
Testing the Lightning in the sandbox test environment is very important since some of the existing Classic features are replaced by Lightning features. Furthermore, testing also ensures that customizations, and integrations work as intended. Testing also covers features that were not covered during Lightning Experience Readiness Check. Exhaustive user acceptance testing and functional testing needs to be performed to understand the impact impact of each change.

Post-migration testing
One of the biggest advantages of Classic to Lightning migration is the innovative features that come as seasonal releases. Every customization and integration needs to be tested whenever a seasonal release is rolled out. Thus, testing cannot be termed a one-time task, but an ongoing process.

As multiple rounds of testing is required during migration and post go-live, doing this manually cannot be considered as a viable solution. All you need is to incorporate automated Salesforce testing. However, all the available test automation tools will not work, since the main stakeholders in Salesforce migration are business users. They are non-technical persons with limited bandwidth. So, you cannot opt for a code based test automation platform.

No-code Salesforce testing platform is what you need.

You need to bring in a no-code Salesforce testing platform with a low learning curve. With low code test automation platform business users can actively participate in testing and can easily validate the business processes.
Also look for self-healing capabilities in a test automation platform since Salesforce is a dynamic application and test automation scripts often become fragile whenever a seasonal release is rolled out.

Rachel Soderberg - Jul 14 '20

Michael Bogan - Jul 1 '20

Rachel Soderberg - Jun 11 '20

Johnny Tordgeman - May 18 '20
