item.title: 一致性哈希 | Consistent Hashing
item.description:
在这篇博客中，我们将讨论什么是一致性哈希以及我们为什么需要使用它以及如何使用它。 让我们首先了解为什么我们需要使用一致性哈希。

为什么要使用一致性哈希？

在我们之前关于系统设计的博客中。 我们定义了一个问题，其中一台服务器无法为我们的大量流量提供服务，而我们需要一些可靠的东西，因此我们考虑水平扩展我们的服务器并在其之上添加一个负载均衡器以将流量平均重定向到每台服务器。
当客户端向负载平衡器发送请求时，负载平衡器使用负载平衡算法将请求路由到其服务器池中的其中一台服务器。

有几种不同的负载平衡算法可用于确定将请求发送到哪个服务器。 一些常见的算法包括：

  • 循环法：负载均衡器轮流向服务器发送请求，将下一个请求发送到列表中的下一个服务器。
  • 最少连接数：负载均衡器将请求发送到活动连接数最少的服务器。
  • 最短响应时间：负载均衡器将请求发送到先前请求的响应时间最短的服务器。
  • 权重越高的服务器收到的请求比例越高。

负载均衡器还可以使用健康检查来监控每台服务器的状态，并将请求仅路由到健康的服务器。 这有助于确保不会将请求发送到已关闭或遇到问题的服务器。

哈希函数

负载平衡器还使用哈希函数将用户重定向到他们之前连接的服务器。 散列函数是一种数学函数，它采用输入值（也称为键）并返回固定大小的输出值（也称为散列或摘要）。

哈希函数很好，以防我们希望在整个操作过程中将用户连接到同一台服务器，以便我们可以根据我们在同一台服务器上为该用户存储的会话状态或缓存更好地为他们提供服务。

如果另一个用户连接，负载均衡器将计算哈希值并决定分配该请求的服务器。 将同一用户分配到同一服务器非常重要，因为我们在服务器上保存了与该用户相关的缓存和其他内容，可以帮助我们更好地为他们提供服务。

这看起来不错，但问题是在服务器池中添加和删除服务器时。

让我们了解它是如何成为一个问题的。

假设由于负载增加，我们添加了一台新服务器，现在我们有 5 台服务器来处理流量。 现在，问题是当用户 A 发出请求时，它将由完全不同的服务器处理，因为我们的服务器增加了，我们的散列函数输出也增加了，因为之前它计算的值考虑了 4 个服务器，但现在我们有 5 个。所以这就是整个问题所在。

添加或删除服务器将导致不同服务器上的用户混洗，这对我们来说可能是个大问题。 那么解决方法是什么？

这就是一致性哈希的用武之地。

什么是一致性哈希？

一致性哈希是一种哈希技术，它允许在哈希表（也称为分布式哈希表或 DHT）中添加或删除节点，而不会显着改变键到节点的映射。

这意味着，我们可以添加和删除服务器，而无需对连接的用户进行大量调整。

在一致性哈希中，所有可能键的集合称为“哈希空间”。 哈希空间通常表示为一个圆圈，键均匀分布在圆圈周围。 这种表示称为“哈希环”。

一旦我们连接了我们的散列空间并像一个环一样重复它，就称为散列环。

分布式系统中的每个节点也在哈希环上分配了一个位置，负责存储落在哈希环一定范围内的key。 节点负责的密钥范围称为其“区域”。

现在，我们已将服务器映射到环上，让我们了解这些服务器将如何处理请求。

每个请求将由它遇到的第一个服务器按顺时针方向处理。 根据哈希函数，r1 位于 s4 和 s1 之间的环上，因此 r1 将由服务器 s1 处理。 当一个新的请求被插入到系统中时，它被散列并放置在散列环上的特定位置。

这看起来不错。 现在，当我们添加和删除服务器时会发生什么。

添加新服务器

如前所述，将新服务器添加到环中只需要重新分配几个密钥。

假设我们添加了一个新服务器，并根据哈希函数将其放置在 s3 和 s4 之间的环上。

当我们添加一个新的服务器 s5 时，它被映射到 s3 和 s4 之间的环上。 所以唯一需要洗牌的请求是 r3。 所以从现在开始，r3 由服务器 s5 处理。

删除服务器

从环中移除服务器也只需要重新分配几个密钥。

假设 s1 出现故障，那么唯一需要重新分配的请求是 r1 。 根据顺时针方向，r1 将由服务器 s2 处理。

这看起来不错，因为如果添加或删除任何服务器，我们已经解决了请求的大量重新分配问题。 但是这种方法也几乎没有问题。

一致性哈希的问题

  • 不均匀的哈希空间。
  • 非均匀请求分布

不均匀的哈希空间
考虑到可以添加新服务器和删除现有服务器，不可能使所有服务器在环上的每个哈希空间的大小都相同。

分配给每个服务器的环上哈希空间的大小可能非常小或相当大。

非均匀请求分布非均匀请求分布是指将大部分请求分布到一台服务器，而很少或没有请求分布到其他服务器。

我们的哈希函数可能会创建结果，使大部分请求仅由一台服务器处理。



原文:

In this blog, we’ll talk about what is consistent hashing and why we need to use it and how it can be used. Let’s first understand why we need to use consistent hashing.

Why Consistent Hashing?

In our previous blog about system design. We define a problem where one server is not able to server our heavy load of traffic and we need something reliable, so we thought of scaling our server horizontally and adding a load balancer on top of it to redirect traffic to each server equally.
When a client sends a request to a load balancer, the load balancer routes the request to one of the servers in its pool of servers using a load balancing algorithm.

There are several different load balancing algorithms that can be used to determine which server to send the request to. Some common algorithms include:

  • Round-robin: The load balancer sends requests to servers in a rotation, sending the next request to the next server in the list.
  • Least connections: The load balancer sends requests to the server with the least number of active connections.
  • Least response time: The load balancer sends requests to the server that has the lowest response time for previous requests.
  • Weighted round-robin: The load balancer sends requests to servers in a rotation, but the rotation is based on the weight assigned to each server. Servers with a higher weight receive a higher proportion of the requests.

The load balancer can also use health checks to monitor the status of each server and route requests only to healthy servers. This helps to ensure that requests are not sent to servers that are down or experiencing issues.

HASH FUNCTION

Load balancers also use a hash function to redirect the user to server which they were connected before. A hash function is a mathematical function that takes an input value (also called a key) and returns a fixed-size output value (also called a hash or a digest).

Hash function are good, in case where we want to connect a user to same server throughout its course of actions so that we can server them better based on the session state or cache we have stored for that user on that same server.

If another user connects, load balancer will calculate the hash value and decide which server to assign that request. It is important to assign the same user to same server as we have cache and other things related to that user saved on the server that can help us to server they better.

This looks good, but the problem is when servers are added and removed from the server pool.

Let’s understand how it is a problem.

Let’s suppose , due to increase in load, we added a new server and now we have 5 servers to serve the traffic. Now, the issue is when user A will make a request, it will be handled by completely different server because our servers increased and so does our hash function output, because before it was calculating the value considering 4 servers but now we have 5. so This is where the whole issue lies.

Adding or Removing a server will result in shuffling the users on different servers and this can be a big problem for us. So what is the solution?

This is where Consistent Hashing comes in.

WHAT IS CONSISTENT HASHING?

Consistent hashing is a hashing technique that allows nodes to be added or removed from a hash table (also called a distributed hash table or DHT) without significantly altering the mapping of keys to nodes.

It means , we can add and remove servers without doing much of the shuffling of connected users.

In consistent hashing, the set of all possible keys is called the “hash space.” The hash space is typically represented as a circle, with the keys distributed evenly around the circle. This representation is called a “hash ring.”

Once we connect our hash space and repesents it like a ring that will be called hash ring.

Each node in the distributed system is also assigned a position on the hash ring, and is responsible for storing the keys that fall within a certain range of the hash ring. The range of keys that a node is responsible for is called its “zone.”

*Using a hash function, we can map servers onto the ring.
*

Now, we have our server mapped on to the ring, let’s understand how the requests will be handled by these servers.

Each request will be handled by the first server it encounter by moving to the clockwise direction. Based on the hash function, r1 is places on the ring between s4 and s1, so r1 will be handled by the server s1. r2 is places on the ring between s2 and s1, so r2 will be handled by the server s2. r3 is places on the ring between s4 and s3, so r3 will be handled by the server s4.

When a new request is inserted into the system, it is hashed and placed on the hash ring at a specific position.

This looks good. Now what will happen when we add and remove servers.

Adding A New Server

Adding a new server to the ring will require only redistribution of few keys as we discussed before.

Let’s suppose we added a new server and based on the hash function , it is placed on the ring between s3 and s4.

When we add a new server s5, it is mapped on the ring between s3 and s4. So the only requests that needs to be shuffled is r3. So from now on, r3 is handled by the server s5.

Removing A Server

Removing a server from the ring will require only redistribution of few keys as well.

Let’s suppose s1 goes down, then the only request that need to redistributed is r1 . Based on the clockwise approach, r1 will be handled by the server s2.

This looks good as we have solved large redistribution of requests if any server is added or removed. But there are few issues with this approach as well.

Issues With Consistent Hashing

  • Uneven hash space.
  • Non uniform request distribution

Uneven hash space
It is impossible to keep the size of each hash space on the ring same for all servers considering that new servers can be added and existing one can be removed.

It is possible that the size of the hash space on the ring assigned to each server is very small or fairly large.

Non uniform request distribution Non uniform request distribution means distribution of most of the requests to one server and little or no request to other servers.

It might happen that our hash function will create results that will make most of the requests to be handled by one server only.

item.title: 低代码平台需要产品设计师/UXUI | Product Designer / UXUI Wanted for Low-Code Platform
item.description:
你相信时间比金钱更宝贵吗？
结果是高效率。

我们正在寻找 UX/UI 产品设计师加入我们的团队，帮助领导参与并与开发人员合作以建立您的愿景。 我们致力于解决通常专注于企业、企业对企业客户的大型软件问题。

作为一名设计师，您将从事以 Web 应用程序为中心的复杂的、工作流驱动的设计。 理想的候选人具有高度的好奇心和对我们客户的同情心，对创造最佳客户体验的热情，以及对挖掘和分析数据和研究以推动从概念到产品发布的最佳设计解决方案的热情。 我们还在寻找具有强大构思、信息架构、原型制作和视觉设计技能的候选人。

您将与我们的前端开发人员合作，使用最新技术打造令人惊叹的体验。 本职位总部设在Yogyakarta, DIY。

设计技巧

  • 角色发展
  • 线框图/交互设计
  • 工作流程图/旅程图
  • 信息架构/分类创建
  • 实施设计系统
  • 更新和维护组件和样式库、设计系统
  • 使用 Figma 构建原型
  • 运行可用性测试

你会做什么

  • 使用 Figma（和 FigJam）布局创意来捕捉设计创意并准备任何支持流程来传达您的想法。
  • 向内部和外部利益相关者展示并捍卫您的设计。
  • 记录您的设计，以便向开发人员清楚地传达需求。
  • 审查实施的设计以确保质量和对规范的遵守。
  • 与其他设计师合作维护产品的模式和样式库。
  • 这份工作最重要的要求是沟通。 当然，最终衡量您的标准是输出的质量和及时性，但我们看到许多有能力的顾问都失败了； 他们失败不是因为他们不会设计，而是因为他们不善于沟通状态或与团队合作以实现共同目标。

我们的追求

  • 注重细节的人，可以提出要求并制作出其他人可以理解的像素完美设计。
  • 一个清晰的沟通者，可以清楚地表达问题并提出具有强烈原因的设计。 如果你不喜欢在人群面前讲话，或者不喜欢与其他人互动，那么这份工作不适合你。
  • 自我激励的人，他们知道自己需要完成工作，不需要持续的监督或监督。
  • 具有以下技术的经验/知识也是非常可取的，但不是必需的：HTML、Javascript、CSS

如果您没有勾选上面的每个方框，我们仍然鼓励您申请。 我们正在建立一支多元化的团队，其技能相互平衡和互补。

自 2013 年以来，RUN System 专门为制造、分销、贸易和服务行业的大中型企业提供 ERP 软件解决方案。
这不是 ERP，它是运行系统： www.youtube.com/watch ?v=5d94VLAVvj4
运行系统记录： www.youtube.com/watch ?v=uiezxm3Q7_Y

你的作品集应展示以用户为中心的设计过程、设计思维以及最终设计解决方案的示例。

立即加入 Run System 👉 s.id/helloken

原文:

Do you believe time is more valuable than money?
Run System helps ordinary companies to have an equal opportunity with "Fortune 2000" companies to implement world-class productivity systems. Big-time efficiency is the result.

We are looking for a UX/UI Product Designer to join our team and help lead engagements and work with developers to get your visions built. We work on big software problems typically focused on enterprise, business-to-business clients.

As a designer you will work on complex, workflow-driven designs centered on web applications. The ideal candidate has a high level of curiosity, and empathy for our customers, a passion for creating the best customer experience, and a zeal for mining and analyzing data and research to drive optimal design solutions from concept to product launch. We’re also looking for a candidate with strong ideation, information architecture, prototyping, and visual design skills.

You will work with our front-end developers to build amazing experiences using the latest technology. This position is headquartered in Yogyakarta, DIY.

Design skills

  • Figma (or Sketch)
  • Persona development
  • Wireframing/interaction design
  • Workflow diagramming/journey mapping
  • Information architecture/taxonomy creation
  • Implementing design system
  • Updating and maintaining component and style libraries, design systems
  • Build prototypes with Figma
  • Run usability tests

What you will do

  • Layout ideas using Figma (and FigJam) to capture the design ideas and prepare any supporting flows to communicate your ideas.
  • Present and defend your designs to both internal and external stakeholders.
  • Document your designs to clearly communicate requirements to the developers.
  • Review implemented designs to ensure quality and adherence to the specifications.
  • Work with other designers to maintain the pattern and style libraries for the product.
  • The most important requirement of the job is communication. Of course, you are ultimately measured on the quality and timeliness of your output, but we have seen many competent consultants fail; they failed not because they could not design, but because they were not good at communicating status or working with the team to achieve common goals.

What we seek

  • Someone with an eye for detail, who can take requirements and produce pixel-perfect designs that others understand.
  • A clear communicator that can articulate problems and present designs with strong why. If you don’t like to speak in front of groups, or don’t like interacting with other humans, then this job isn’t for you.
  • Self-motivated individuals who know they need to get a job done and do not require constant hand-holding or oversight.
  • Experience/knowledge in the following technologies are also highly desirable but not required: HTML, Javascript, CSS

If you don’t tick every box above, we’d still encourage you to apply. We’re building a diverse team whose skills balance and complement one another.

Global Sukses Solusi was initiated in 2013 and operates under the brand name “RUN System”. Since 2013, RUN System has specialized in providing ERP software solutions for medium to large-scale businesses in the manufacturing, distribution, trading, and service industries.
The main strength of RUN System is our extensive expertise and experience in planning, designing, building, and implementing Information Technology Solutions for all integrated business processes.

It's not ERP, It's Run System: www.youtube.com/watch?v=5d94VLAVvj4
Run System track record: www.youtube.com/watch?v=uiezxm3Q7_Y

Run System is an Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) employer and does not discriminate based on race, color, national origin, religion, gender, age, veteran status, political affiliation, sexual orientation, marital status, or disability.

*Please include your portfolio in your application. Your portfolio should show examples of the user-centered design process, design thinking, as well as your final design solutions.

Join Run System NOW 👉 s.id/helloken

Timeless DEV post...

Git Concepts I Wish I Knew Years Ago

The most used technology by developers is not Javascript.

It's not Python or HTML.

It hardly even gets mentioned in interviews or listed as a pre-requisite for jobs.

I'm talking about Git and version control of course.

Richard Torres - Sep 18 '22

Albert⚔️⛓️👨‍💻 - Sep 11 '22

Make all posts by kenniko less visible

kenniko consistently posts content that violates DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.
